questions.text:
Implement a basic multi-signature wallet in Solidity with the following requirements:
• The constructor receives an array of owner addresses and a required number of confirmations (M out of N).
• Any owner can submit a transaction (to, value, data).
• Other owners can confirm; once M confirmations are reached, anyone can execute the transaction.
• A transaction cannot be executed more than once.
• Include appropriate access control and event emissions.
questions.explanation:
3 marks - Correct state variables (owners mapping, transaction struct, confirmations mapping, transactionCount).
          2 marks - `submitTransaction` with event, `confirmTransaction` with proper confirmation logic.
          3 marks - `executeTransaction` with require checks (confirmCount >= required, not executed, valid to address). Reset confirmations or mark executed.
          2 marks - Security: onlyOwner modifier for submitting? (any owner can submit), require checks, prevent re-execution. Emit events.
          Note: Partial code with clear communication of missing parts can still earn up to 7 marks if logic is sound.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marking Scheme (10 marks)

3 marks - Correct state variables (owners mapping, transaction struct, confirmations mapping, transactionCount).
2 marks - `submitTransaction` with event, `confirmTransaction` with proper confirmation logic.
3 marks - `executeTransaction` with require checks (confirmCount >= required, not executed, valid to address). Reset confirmations or mark executed.
2 marks - Security: onlyOwner modifier for submitting? (any owner can submit), require checks, prevent re-execution. Emit events.
Note: Partial code with clear communication of missing parts can still earn up to 7 marks if logic is sound.

questions.text: Build a staking contract where users stake an ERC-20 token and earn reward tokens at a fixed rate of 10 tokens per second, distributed proportionally among all stakers. Functions: `stake(uint256 amount)`, `withdraw(uint256 amount)`, `getReward()`. Use the “reward per token” accumulator pattern to avoid looping over stakers.
questions.explanation:
2 marks - State variables: `rewardPerTokenStored`, `userRewardPerTokenPaid`, `rewards`, `totalSupply` of staked tokens.
          2 marks - `updateReward` modifier / internal function that updates global and user reward accumulators before any stake/unstake/getReward.
          2 marks - `stake` and `withdraw` correctly update user balance and totalSupply, invoke `updateReward`.
          2 marks - `getReward` transfers accumulated reward to user, resets rewards mapping.
          2 marks - Security: nonReentrant on withdraw/getReward, use SafeERC20 for token transfers, proper visibility.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marking Scheme (10 marks)

2 marks - State variables: `rewardPerTokenStored`, `userRewardPerTokenPaid`, `rewards`, `totalSupply` of staked tokens.
2 marks - `updateReward` modifier / internal function that updates global and user reward accumulators before any stake/unstake/getReward.
2 marks - `stake` and `withdraw` correctly update user balance and totalSupply, invoke `updateReward`.
2 marks - `getReward` transfers accumulated reward to user, resets rewards mapping.
2 marks - Security: nonReentrant on withdraw/getReward, use SafeERC20 for token transfers, proper visibility.

questions.text: Create a vault where an owner can deposit Ether, and a beneficiary can withdraw the entire balance only after a specific unlock timestamp. The owner can also perform an emergency withdrawal of all funds at any time. Include events for deposits and withdrawals.
questions.explanation:
2 marks - Constructor sets owner, beneficiary, unlockTime.
          2 marks - `deposit() payable` onlyOwner, emits event.
          3 marks - `withdraw()` only beneficiary, `require(block.timestamp >= unlockTime, "too early")`, transfers entire balance.
          2 marks - `emergencyWithdraw()` onlyOwner, transfers balance regardless of time.
          1 mark - Events: Deposited, Withdrawn.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marking Scheme (10 marks)

2 marks - Constructor sets owner, beneficiary, unlockTime.
2 marks - `deposit() payable` onlyOwner, emits event.
3 marks - `withdraw()` only beneficiary, `require(block.timestamp >= unlockTime, "too early")`, transfers entire balance.
2 marks - `emergencyWithdraw()` onlyOwner, transfers balance regardless of time.
1 mark - Events: Deposited, Withdrawn.

questions.text: Design a decentralised token exchange (like Uniswap) that runs on MST blockchain. Discuss the smart contract architecture, the user flow for a swap, how you'd handle liquidity provision, and how you'd integrate MST-specific features (SARAL, WASMify, low fees). Draw a rough architecture diagram (describe it textually).
questions.explanation:
3 marks - Clear contract separation: Factory, Pair (or Pool), Router. Explain constant product AMM.
          2 marks - User flow: Wallet connection (BridgeKey) → Router swap call → Pair executes transfer → events for indexing.
          2 marks - Liquidity: LP tokens, fee accrual, impermanent loss mention.
          2 marks - MST specifics: Use SARAL for mobile wallet login; WASMify for complex price computation off-chain; low fees enable micro-swaps.
          1 mark - Security: reentrancy guards, slippage protection (`minAmountOut`), Oracle usage for safety.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marking Scheme (10 marks)

3 marks - Clear contract separation: Factory, Pair (or Pool), Router. Explain constant product AMM.
2 marks - User flow: Wallet connection (BridgeKey) → Router swap call → Pair executes transfer → events for indexing.
2 marks - Liquidity: LP tokens, fee accrual, impermanent loss mention.
2 marks - MST specifics: Use SARAL for mobile wallet login; WASMify for complex price computation off-chain; low fees enable micro-swaps.
1 mark - Security: reentrancy guards, slippage protection (`minAmountOut`), Oracle usage for safety.

questions.text: Design an NFT marketplace on MST where creators can mint NFTs (ERC-721) and list them for sale. Implement royalties: every secondary sale sends a percentage to the original creator. Discuss how you'd store metadata (IPFS), handle offers, and ensure the marketplace fee is configurable. Mention how you'd use MST's low fees for a better creator experience.
questions.explanation:
3 marks - Contract design: NFT contract (ERC-721) + Marketplace contract (listing, buying, bidding). Royalty using EIP-2981.
          2 marks - Metadata: IPFS link stored in `tokenURI`. Frontend fetches JSON.
          2 marks - Listing flow: seller approves marketplace, lists with price. Buyer pays, marketplace transfers NFT, distributes fee and royalty.
          2 marks - MST: low gas enables cheap listing/cancel; SARAL for creator onboarding without seed phrase; WASMify for off-chain auction logic.
          1 mark - Security: reentrancy guard on purchase; ensure royalty payment cannot be bypassed.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marking Scheme (10 marks)

3 marks - Contract design: NFT contract (ERC-721) + Marketplace contract (listing, buying, bidding). Royalty using EIP-2981.
2 marks - Metadata: IPFS link stored in `tokenURI`. Frontend fetches JSON.
2 marks - Listing flow: seller approves marketplace, lists with price. Buyer pays, marketplace transfers NFT, distributes fee and royalty.
2 marks - MST: low gas enables cheap listing/cancel; SARAL for creator onboarding without seed phrase; WASMify for off-chain auction logic.
1 mark - Security: reentrancy guard on purchase; ensure royalty payment cannot be bypassed.
